title: Herb-drug interactions among commonly used psychoactive substances by healthcare students

extraction.extraction_notes: Abstract discusses potential for herb-drug interactions and states documented interactions are rare; no specific herb-drug interactions, herbs, drugs, mechanisms, or quantitative data are described. Study objective is prevalence assessment among students.

title: Analysis on the Status Quo and Requirement of Medication in Gestational Women and Effect of Pharmaceutical Care

extraction.extraction_notes: Survey/intervention study on medication use in pregnant women; mentions use of traditional Chinese medicines and that medication consultation covered 'drug interaction', but no specific herb(s), drug(s), or interaction details (mechanism, direction, or quantitative data) are reported in the abstract.

title: Preliminary study of the effects of β-elemene on breast cancer stem cells in cell line MCF-7/ADM

interactions.evidence_quote: βELE inhibited obviously microspheres formation ratio,decreased mRNA and protein levels of BCRP and P-gp.

extraction.extraction_notes: In MCF-7/ADM cells (adriamycin-resistant), β-elemene reduced BCRP and P-gp expression in vitro; this implies potential reversal of adriamycin resistance (increased intracellular effect) via downregulation of efflux transporters, but no direct PK or efficacy measurements were reported.

title: A study to determine the knowledge and level of awareness of medical undergraduates about herbal medicines and herb-drug

extraction.extraction_notes: Cross-sectional survey of medical students reporting awareness and familiarity with certain herbs (tulsi, neem, turmeric, aloe, ashwagandha, ginger) but no specific herb-drug interactions, mechanisms, or clinical PK/PD data were described.

title: EFFECTIVNESS OF COMPLEMENTARY AND ALTERNATIVE THERAPY FOR HIV/AIDS -

extraction.extraction_notes: Narrative overview of complementary and alternative therapies used by people with HIV and a general statement that safety risks and potential drug interactions with highly active antiretroviral therapy (HAART) are frequently ignored; no specific herbs, drugs, or interactions are described in the abstract.

title: INHIBITORY EFFECT OF AQUEOUS ROOT EXTRACT OF DECALEPIS HAMILTONII ON rOAT1

interactions.herb_active_compound: phenolic acids and other root-specific compounds
interactions.drug_class: OAT1 substrate / renal plasma flow probe
interactions.evidence_quote: The results shows significant decrease in CL & k 10 , and significant elevation in t 1/2 , AUC 0- & V ss of PAH in test group compared to control group clearly indicates the cumulative inhibitory effect of phenolic acids present in the AREDH.

interactions.herb_active_compound: phenolic acids and other root-specific compounds
interactions.evidence_quote: Further, the co-administration of AREDH with any hOAT1 substrate, results in decrease in clearance and increase in the bioavailability and half-life of the substrate drugs.

extraction.extraction_notes: In vivo rat study demonstrating rOAT1 inhibition by Decalepis hamiltonii aqueous root extract (AREDH) with increased PAH exposure; authors extrapolate to potential interactions with human OAT1 substrate drugs. No quantitative percent changes or sample size reported in abstract.

title: THE NEED FOR HERBOVIGILANCE

extraction.extraction_notes: Narrative communication/review outlining concepts of herb–drug interactions and need for herbovigilance; no specific herb-drug pair, effect, mechanism, or quantitative interaction data reported.

title: A role for physicians in ethnopharmacology and drug discovery.

extraction.extraction_notes: Narrative discussing roles of physicians in ethnopharmacology and recommending clinical studies on herb–drug interactions, but no specific herb-drug interactions or herbs/drugs are described in the abstract.

title: Lack of pharmacokinetic interaction between St. John's wort and prednisone.

interactions.evidence_quote: Twenty-eight days of SJW treatment resulted in no significant alterations in the pharmacokinetic parameters for prednisone or prednisolone.
Twenty-eight days of SJW treatment resulted in no significant alterations in the pharmacokinetic parameters for prednisone or prednisolone.

title: Tanshinone IIA increases the bystander effect of herpes simplex virus thymidine kinase/ganciclovir gene therapy via enha

interactions.specific_target: enhancement of gap junctional intercellular communication (connexins Cx26, Cx43)
interactions.evidence_quote: Tan IIA increased GJIC in B16 melanoma cells, leading to more efficient GCV-induced bystander killing in cells stably expressing HSV-tk. Additionally, in vivo experiments demonstrated that tumors in mice with 10% HSV-tk positive B16 cells and 90% wild-type B16 cells became smaller following treatment with the combination of GCV and Tan IIA as compared to GCV or Tan IIA alone.

extraction.extraction_notes: Interaction is pharmacodynamic: Tanshinone IIA (a plant-derived compound) increases gap junction coupling (Cx26/Cx43) and thereby enhances the HSV-tk/ganciclovir bystander killing. Evidence includes in vitro cell data and in vivo mouse tumor reduction; primary in vivo evidence chosen as evidence_type.

title: Effects of berberine on the pharmacokinetics of losartan and its metabolite EXP3174 in rats and its mechanism.

interactions.evidence_quote: The Cmax (1.26 ± 0.37 versus 1.96 ± 0.45 mg/L) and the AUC(0-t) (8.25 ± 0.89 versus 12.70 ± 1.42 mg·h/L) of losartan were significantly (p < 0.05) increased by BBR compared to the control.

interactions.drug_class: active metabolite of losartan (angiotensin II receptor blocker)
interactions.evidence_quote: The Cmax (0.97 ± 0.15 versus 0.77 ± 0.06 mg/L) of EXP3174 was significantly decreased compared to the control (p < 0.05). The Tmax of EXP3174 was decreased significantly (p < 0.05) from 8.14 ± 0.36 to 3.33 ± 0.28 h.

extraction.extraction_notes: Rat PK study showed increased losartan exposure and decreased EXP3174 Cmax/Tmax after berberine; rat liver microsome data showed increased metabolic stability of losartan (37.4 to 59.6 min), supporting CYP inhibition (authors infer CYP3A4 or CYP2C9 involvement).

title: The effect of Chinese herbs and its effective components on coronary heart disease through PPARs-PGC1α pathway.

extraction.extraction_notes: DanQi pill (DQP) and its components (Salvianolic acids, Panax notoginseng saponins) were reported to suppress expression of cytochrome P450 in a rat ischemic heart model, which could imply potential CYP-mediated herb-drug interactions. However, no co-administered pharmaceutical drug was reported to interact with the herbs (clofibrate was used only as a positive-control treatment group, not co-administered), and no pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic interaction with a specific drug was measured or described in the abstract. Therefore no specific herb-drug interaction can be extracted.

title: The Synergistic Effects of Astragalus mongholicus and Salvia miltiorrhiza on Coronary Heart Disease Identi

extraction.extraction_notes: Study investigates synergistic effects of two herbs (Astragalus mongholicus and Salvia miltiorrhiza) in a rat model of myocardial infarction; no conventional pharmaceutical drugs or herb-drug interactions are described.

title: Mechanistic insights into the amelioration effects of diabetic cardiomyopathy by berberine: an integrated systems pharma

extraction.extraction_notes: Mechanistic/effect study of berberine (Coptidis Rhizoma) on diabetic cardiomyopathy using systems pharmacology and diabetic mouse experiments; no co-administered conventional drugs or herb-drug interactions are described or implied.

title: Hepatoprotective diterpenes from the nodes of Pinus massoniana

title: Quality of manufacturer provided information on safety and efficacy claims for dietary supplements for colonic health

extraction.extraction_notes: Survey and literature review identified that potential adverse events and drug-supplement interactions exist (mostly case reports, animal studies, or in vitro experiments), but the abstract does not specify any particular herb, compound, or drug interactions with quantitative or specific details; thus no extractable HDIs.

extraction.extraction_notes: Study compares pharmacokinetics of the herbal constituent rhein in two traditional decoctions (DCQD vs XCQD) in rats; no conventional pharmaceutical drug is involved. Observed differences reflect herb–herb/formulation effects, not herb–drug interactions.

title: Reversal of multidrug resistance by natural substances from plants

interactions.evidence_quote: Capsanthin and capsorubin, carotenoids isolated from paprika, were identified as potent P-gp inhibitors
lycopene, lutein, antheraxanthin and violaxanthin induced moderate effects
Among flavonoids, effective modulators were rotenone, chrysin, phloretin and sakuranetin

extraction.extraction_notes: All reported interactions are in vitro observations of plant-derived compounds or extracts inhibiting MDR efflux transporters (P-gp and/or MRP1) leading to reversal of multidrug resistance and potential increased efficacy of anticancer drugs when combined. No quantitative PK parameters or human/animal data provided in abstract.

title: Interaction of green tea polyphenol epigallocatechin-3-gallate with sunitinib: Potential risk of diminished sunitinib bi

interactions.evidence_quote: The AUC0-∞ and C max of plasma sunitinib were markedly reduced by co-administration of EGCG to rats. ... We noticed that a precipitate was formed when the solutions of sunitinib and EGCG were mixed under both neutral and acidic conditions. Sticky semisolid contents were found in the stomachs of sunitinib and EGCG co-administrated mice.

extraction.extraction_notes: In vitro precipitate formation and in vivo rodent data show marked reductions in sunitinib AUC and Cmax, consistent with reduced GI bioavailability due to physical interaction/precipitation. No quantitative percent changes or human PK data reported.

title: Effect of pomegranate juice on intestinal transport and pharmacokinetics of nitrendipine in rats

interactions.evidence_quote: There was significant increase in effective permeability, absorption rate constant and fraction of drug absorbed in the pretreated group when compared with the control group, probably due to inhibition of the P-glycoprotein- mediated efflux of the drug by PJ.

These results suggest that PJ inhibits the intestinal metabolism of nitrendipine without inhibiting the hepatic metabolism in rats. In comparison with control, PJ treatment significantly increased the are under the concentration-time curve of oral nitrendipine. The peak plasm concentration of nitrendipine was also significantly increased by PJ.

extraction.extraction_notes: Integrative bioinformatics study mapping Si-Wu-Tang ingredients to targets and noting overlap with targets of 2 FDA-approved and 39 experimental drugs; no specific herb-drug interactions (no drug names, effects, directions, or mechanistic data) are reported in the abstract.

title: Plant Phenols as Antibiotic Boosters: In Vitro Interaction of Olive Leaf Phenols with Ampicillin

interactions.herb_active_compound: mixture of phenolic compounds (oleuropein, hydroxytyrosol, caffeic acid, verbascoside, luteolin 7-O-glucoside)
interactions.evidence_quote: In combination with ampicillin, OLE ... showed additive effects.
In combination with ampicillin, ... caffeic acid ... showed additive effects.
In combination with ampicillin, ... verbascoside ... showed additive effects.
In combination with ampicillin, ... oleuropein showed additive effects.
Synergistic interaction was observed between ampicillin and hydroxytyrosol.

extraction.extraction_notes: All reported interactions were observed in vitro against E. coli and S. aureus: OLE, caffeic acid, verbascoside and oleuropein showed additive effects with ampicillin; hydroxytyrosol showed synergistic enhancement. No PK data reported.

title: Effects of Herba Erigerontis injection on pharmacodynamics and pharmacokinetics of warfarin in rats in vivo

interactions.evidence_quote: Pharmacokinetic study manifested that Cmax, AUC and t1/2 prolonged significantly (P <.01) for R/S-warfarin in presence of HEI. Low (3.6 mL/kg), medium (7.2 mL/kg) and high (10.8 mL/kg) doses of HEI could decrease plasma protein binding rate of warfarin significantly (P <.01).
Pharmacodynamic study demonstrated that both HEI alone and co-administered with warfarin could increase PT and INR values significantly (P <.01), whereas the APTT values were unaffected (P >.05).

The area under the curve (AUC) of R- and S-WF in the CX-2 group was a significantly higher value compared to the control (77.54 vs. 35.27 mg.h/L for R-WF and 316.26 vs. 40.16 mg.h/L for S-WF; p < 0.05; Kruskal-Wallis method).

extraction.extraction_notes: Interaction observed in Wistar rats with higher-dose Curcuma xanthorrhiza extract (CX-2, 18 mg/kg BW) co-administered with warfarin (WF 1 mg/kg). Reported AUCs: R-WF 77.54 vs 35.27 mg·h/L and S-WF 316.26 vs 40.16 mg·h/L (p<0.05). Authors also reported concentration increases from day 7 to 9 of 16% (R-WF) and 27% (S-WF). title: Improvement of Astragalin on Spermatogenesis in Oligoasthenozoospermia Mouse Induced by Cyclophosphamide

interactions.specific_target: apoptosis- and steroidogenesis-related pathways (AKT1, BCL-2, CASPASE3/9, STAR, CYP11A1, PRKACB)
interactions.evidence_quote: Male mice were intraperitoneally injected by CTX (50 mg/kg), and astragalin (30 mg/kg) was given via oral gavage once daily. Astragalin also significantly reversed the reduction in body weight, reproductive organs index, and sperm parameters (sperm concentration, viability, and motility) induced by CTX, and restored testicular abnormal histopathologic morphology induced by CTX. Furthermore, astragalin dramatically rescued the gene expression related to spermatogenesis (AKT1, BCL-2, CASPASE9, CASPASE3, MAPKAPK2, RPS6KA5, STAR, and PRKACB), and increased the level of testosterone by improving related proteins (STAR, CYP11A1, PRKACB) for oligoasthenozoospermia induced by CTX.

extraction.extraction_notes: In a mouse model, astragalin mitigated cyclophosphamide-induced testicular/spermatogenic toxicity via modulation of anti-apoptotic and steroidogenesis-related genes/proteins. No pharmacokinetic data reported.

title: Albumin's Influence towards Hepatic Metabolism and Metabolism-Related Drug-Drug Interaction with Aurantio-Obtusin

The addition of BSA significantly decreased the inhibition potential of aurantio-obtusin towards ... UGT1A3
but not affected the activity of UGT1A9

extraction.extraction_notes: In vitro human liver microsomes and recombinant UGT systems showed aurantio-obtusin (from Semen Cassiae) inhibits UGT1A1 and UGT1A3; addition of bovine serum albumin (BSA) decreased the inhibition potential for UGT1A1/1A3 and decreased aurantio-obtusin metabolic velocity at 50 µM. No specific conventional drugs were named; implications are potential altered exposure of drugs metabolized by these UGT isoforms, modulated by albumin.

title: Potential Drug-Drug and Herb-Drug Interactions in Patients With Cancer: A Prospective Study of Medication Surveillance

title: Inhibition of Cytochrome P450 Activities by Extracts of Hyptis verticillata Jacq.: Assessment for Potential HERB-Drug In

interactions.herb_active_compound: multiple lignans and triterpenes (identified in extract)

Individual screening of these phytochemicals against the activity of CYP1A2 identified yatein as a moderate inhibitor (71.9 mu M), likely to contribute to the plant extract's potent bioactivity.

extraction.extraction_notes: In vitro inhibition of multiple CYP enzymes by Hyptis verticillata extracts and identification of yatein as a moderate CYP1A2 inhibitor imply potential for increased exposure of drugs metabolized by these CYPs; no specific clinical drugs or in vivo data reported.
